key_insight: A large autoregressive transformer can generate catalyst structures conditioned on both categorical and continuous targets, enabling controllable inverse design rather than brute-force screening.
engineering_relevance: This approach could substantially speed reaction-targeted catalyst discovery by producing valid, optimized candidates with targeted properties in a single generation pipeline.
method: Pretrained a GPT-style generative model on 133 million catalyst structures.
Fine-tuned on ~460,000 optimized structures with categorical labels and binding energies.
Used numerical embeddings to condition generation on discrete and continuous properties within one autoregressive framework.
evidence_signal: The model reached 98% structural validity, 95% optimization validity, 93% joint match for adsorbate type and composition, and improved binding-energy match and screening efficiency by 1.5–4×.
watch_item: Condition fidelity for binding energy was only ~20%, so practical deployment may still require tighter property control and validation on new catalyst families.
paper_title: Toward Controllable Catalyst Inverse Design via Large-Scale Autoregressive Pretraining
